text.title: privacy-compliance Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for privacy-compliance as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent claims "GA4 is GDPR-compliant" as a conclusion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is a marketing claim, not a legal determination. The correct output is the gating mechanism applied (consent gate + Consent Mode v2 + EU region configuration).

failure-mode.description: Agent writes banner copy with highlighted Accept and hidden Reject button.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs default to UX patterns optimized for conversion, not consent parity; lock UI parity in the template and do not let the agent restyle.

failure-mode.description: Agent claims hashing emails/IDs is sufficient anonymization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hashing without a rotation policy is reversible; only salted hashing with key rotation can reduce re-identification risk, and even then GDPR may still apply.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses Accept-Language or other client-side signal for EU/US/CA region detection.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Region detection must use a server-trusted GeoIP signal; client-side headers are user-controlled and not legally reliable for jurisdiction determination.
